summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special meeting to address a 2025 budget amendment correction and to consider hiring a service department employee. The board rescinded a prior budget resolution containing typos related to the Station 18 rebuilding fund and replaced it with a corrected version. After an executive session, the board approved the hiring of Aaron Pruitt as a part-time service worker.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously
Motion to rescind resolution R-030-2025 and replace it with resolution R-035-2025 passed unanimously
Motion to adjourn into executive session at 8:32 a.m. passed unanimously
Motion to hire Aaron Pruitt as part-time service worker effective 12-18-2025 at $19.25/hour on a one-year probationary period passed unanimously
Motion to adjourn at 8:54 a.m. passed unanimously
action_items: Director Meadows to process hiring of Aaron Pruitt as part-time service worker effective 12-18-2025
key_amounts: $19.25 per hour salary for new part-time service worker Aaron Pruitt
$2.5 million received from the state of Ohio in 2024 for Station 18 rebuilding fund (incorrectly listed as 2025 revenue in prior resolution)
Over $2 million was erroneously expected as 2025 state revenue in the prior resolution
notable_topics: 2025 budget amendment correction (resolution R-035-2025 replacing R-030-2025)
Hiring of part-time service department worker
Acknowledgment of recent storm response efforts
attendees: Chief Myers (mentioned but absent due to structure fire)
notes: Chief Myers had to leave the meeting to respond to a structure fire, so another staff member presented the budget resolution item. Trustee Hodges expressed gratitude to all departments for their storm response work. The meeting was brief, lasting approximately 24 minutes (8:30 a.m. to 8:54 a.m.).
title: BOT Special Meeting 12-17-25
